# agent-help

agent-help is MCP-lite for CLIs: add --agent-help and --agent-out so AI agents can discover, invoke, and parse a command-line tool without an MCP server, plugin, or runtime skill.

Homepage: https://zate.github.io/agent-help
Repository: https://github.com/Zate/agent-help

Important distinction:
- This file orients agents and humans who are learning about the project.
- llms-full.txt teaches a coding agent how to IMPLEMENT agent-help in a CLI.
- An agent USING an already agent-help-enabled CLI should not need this file, llms-full.txt, the spec, or a skill.

Runtime idea:
1. Agent runs: tool --help
2. Agent sees: LLM agent? Use --agent-help for token-optimized usage.
3. Agent runs: tool --agent-help
4. Agent follows the compact records to call commands.
5. Agent uses --agent-out for dense structured results when available.

Audience split:
- humans: default output and --help
- software: --json
- agents: --agent-help and --agent-out

Core CLI convention:
- tool --agent-help returns an AHF command index.
- tool subcmd --agent-help returns AHF command detail.
- tool subcmd args --agent-out returns an AHF ok/err envelope plus dense result body.
- --agent-help is trailing in documented examples.

Tiny --agent-help shape:
- one record per line; first token is record type
- ah1/cmd/more? for index
- ah2/use/arg/flag/ex for detail
- err/hint/use for recoverable invocation errors

Tiny --agent-out shape:
- ok <kind> [key=value...] status line
- dense rows/object result body, usually TOON
- next "..." exact follow-up command when useful

Implementation resources:
- Brief for coding agents: https://zate.github.io/agent-help/llms-full.txt
- Detailed implementer contract: https://zate.github.io/agent-help/spec.html
- Examples: https://zate.github.io/agent-help/examples/
